response.justification: The role requires 8 years of C++ programming as a hard minimum, and the candidate has roughly 6 months of C/C++ exposure (National Instruments, Dec 2014–Jun 2015) with the rest of their career centered on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. While they have strong software craftsmanship, testing discipline, and some distributed-systems exposure (Sociomantic), they do not meet the core technical bar for a C++-centric cloud compute platform tech lead role, and there is no evidence of leading cloud/DevOps engineers at the scale implied.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with strong testing/TDD and craftsmanship culture
Some high-throughput, low-latency distributed-systems work at Sociomantic (millions of requests/sec)
Demonstrated mentoring/teaching via workshops, talks, and course creation
concerns: Far below the 8-year C++ minimum; effectively no recent C++ experience
No clear production cloud compute platform architecture ownership
No explicit technical leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers

key_factors.factor: Limited cloud compute platform architecture ownership
Strong testing, mentoring, and distributed-systems exposure
